comment_text.sn36.22:2.15: Also at MN 137:8.1, MN 140:10.1 and AN 3.61:12.1. Outside of this context, upavicāra is only found in AN 6.52, where women are “preoccupied” with adornments and thieves are “preoccupied” with a hiding place.

root_text.sn36.22:1.4: Dvepi mayā, bhikkhave, vedanā vuttā pariyāyena;
root_text.sn36.22:2.5: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā—
root_text.sn36.22:2.15: Cha somanassūpavicārā, cha domanassūpavicārā, cha upekkhūpavicārā—
root_text.sn36.22:2.18: Cha gehasitāni somanassāni, cha nekkhammasitāni somanassāni, cha gehasitāni domanassāni, cha nekkhammasitāni domanassāni, cha gehasitā upekkhā, cha nekkhammasitā upekkhā—
root_text.sn36.22:2.21: Atītā chattiṁsa vedanā, anāgatā chattiṁsa vedanā, paccuppannā chattiṁsa vedanā—

translation_text.sn36.22:0.2: 3. The Explanation of the Hundred and Eight
translation_text.sn36.22:0.3: The Explanation of the Hundred and Eight
translation_text.sn36.22:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you an exposition of the teaching on the hundred and eight.
translation_text.sn36.22:1.3: And what is the exposition of the teaching on the hundred and eight?
translation_text.sn36.22:1.4: Mendicants, in one explanation I’ve spoken of two feelings. In another explanation I’ve spoken of three feelings, or five, six, eighteen, thirty-six, or a hundred and eight feelings.
translation_text.sn36.22:2.1: And what are the two feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.3: These are called the two feelings.
translation_text.sn36.22:2.4: And what are the three feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.7: And what are the five feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.8: The faculties of pleasure, pain, happiness, sadness, and equanimity. …
translation_text.sn36.22:2.10: And what are the six feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.11: Feeling born of eye contact … ear contact … nose contact … tongue contact … body contact …
translation_text.sn36.22:2.14: And what are the eighteen feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.15: There are six preoccupations with happiness, six preoccupations with sadness, and six preoccupations with equanimity. …
translation_text.sn36.22:2.17: And what are the thirty-six feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.18: Six kinds of domestic happiness and six kinds of renunciate happiness. Six kinds of domestic sadness and six kinds of renunciate sadness. Six kinds of domestic equanimity and six kinds of renunciate equanimity. …
translation_text.sn36.22:2.20: And what are the hundred and eight feelings?
translation_text.sn36.22:2.21: Thirty six feelings in the past, future, and present.
translation_text.sn36.22:2.22: These are called the hundred and eight feelings.
translation_text.sn36.22:2.23: This is the exposition of the teaching on the hundred and eight.”
